findings.text: Chronic LBBB since 11/2024. Baseline ECG 6/28/2026: sinus 94, LBBB unchanged, QTc 452, no Sgarbossa.
Apixaban 5 mg BID — active since 2023-08-04, AFib, CHA2DS2-VASc 4. Last dose timing NOT documented in ED note.
Sildenafil 50 mg taken ~11 hours prior to arrival, per EMS report at 08:55. Within 24-hour nitrate contraindication window.
Cardiology 1/2026: pharmacologic stress imaging ordered but not completed (patient deferred). Note explicitly recommends low threshold for repeat workup with exertional symptoms.
Prior workup 6/28/2026 negative — but that was for AT-REST pain, not exertional. Cardiology framed it as insufficient for exertional symptoms.
hs-Troponin T resulted critical high: 62 ng/L (ref <14). First troponin of the encounter. Confirms myocardial injury.
Outside 2024 stress echo faxed in: peak-stress inferolateral hypokinesis, equivocal for ischemia; no follow-up testing despite recommendation. Corroborates cardiology's flagged incomplete workup.
Today's CXR final attending read: WIDENED MEDIASTINUM compared to 6/28/2026 baseline. Stable mild cardiomegaly. Recommend clinical correlation for aortic pathology.

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast: intimal flap in ascending aorta extending from root through arch into descending aorta. Stanford Type A / DeBakey I acute dissection. No tamponade. Coronary ostia preserved.
